question: When did South Sudan become an independent country?
reference_answer: South Sudan gained independence on 9 July 2011, becoming the world's newest internationally recognized sovereign state [S1].

must_support.claim: South Sudan became an independent sovereign state in 2011.
must_support.supporting_quote: Sudan achieved independence from joint British and Egyptian rule on 1 January 1956, ending the condominium administration that had governed the territory for more than half a century.

must_not_assert: South Sudan gained independence on 9 July 2011.
